# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React TypeScript frontend application built with Vite, featuring a comprehensive HR management system. The application follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Tech Stack
- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, Context API for global state
- **UI Components**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod validation
- **Rich Text**: Tiptap editor for policy documents
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions)
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization

## Application Structure

### Key Directories
- `src/components/` - Reusable UI components organized by feature
- `src/pages/` - Route-level components (one per route)
- `src/hooks/` - Custom React hooks for business logic
- `src/lib/` - Utility functions and configurations
- `src/integrations/` - External service integrations (Supabase)
- `supabase/migrations/` - Database schema definitions
- `supabase/functions/` - Serverless edge functions

### Component Organization
- `components/ui/` - Design system components (buttons, forms, etc.)
- `components/layout/` - App shell components (sidebar, topbar, layout)
- `components/[feature]/` - Feature-specific components

## Data Flow & State Management

### Authentication
- Supabase Auth handles user sessions
- Custom `useAuth` hook manages user state and permissions
- Role-based access control with `canAccess` utility
- Protected routes redirect unauthorized users

### Data Fetching
- React Query manages all server state
- Queries are cached and automatically invalidated
- Optimistic updates for better user experience
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates

### Form Management
- React Hook Form for performance and DX
- Zod schemas define validation rules
- Type-safe form data with TypeScript inference
- File uploads with progress tracking

## Key Business Logic

### Role-Based Permissions
- CEO: Full access to all features
- HR Manager: Employee management, evaluations, payroll
- Team Lead: Team evaluations, project management
- Employee: Limited access to own data and evaluations

### Evaluation System
- Quarterly evaluations: Formal performance reviews
- Daily evaluations: Peer feedback and mentoring
- Visibility rules: Managers see all, employees see relevant evaluations
- Timeline view: Chronological evaluation history

### Payroll Processing
- Attendance-based calculations
- Overtime and allowance tracking
- PDF generation for payslips
- Automated processing with Supabase functions

### Invoice Management
- Client invoicing with line items
- PDF generation and email delivery
- Status tracking and payment management

## Database Schema

### Core Tables
- `companies` - Multi-tenant company data
- `employees` - User profiles with role assignments
- `roles` - Permission definitions
- `departments` - Organizational structure
- `projects` - Project management
- `clients` - Client relationship management
- `invoices` - Billing and invoicing
- `evaluations` - Performance reviews
- `daily_evaluations` - Daily feedback
- `payroll` - Salary processing
- `loans` - Employee loan tracking
- `hr_policies` - Policy document management

### Security
- Row Level Security (RLS) policies
- Company-based data isolation
- Role-based access control
- Secure file storage with Supabase Storage

## Development Patterns

### Component Patterns
- Compound components for complex UI
- Custom hooks for reusable logic
- TypeScript generics for flexible APIs
- CSS custom properties for theming

### Data Patterns
- React Query for declarative data fetching
- Optimistic updates for immediate feedback
- Error boundaries for graceful failure handling
- Loading states with skeleton components

### Routing Patterns
- Nested routes for complex page structures
- Route guards for authorization
- Lazy loading for performance
- Search params for state management

## Performance Optimizations

### Build Optimizations
- Vite's fast HMR and optimized builds
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Tree shaking for unused code elimination
- Asset optimization and compression

### Runtime Optimizations
- React Query caching and background updates
- Virtual scrolling for large lists
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- Bundle analysis and monitoring

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing
- Vitest for fast, modern testing
- React Testing Library for component testing
- Mock Service Worker for API mocking
- Test utilities for common patterns

### E2E Testing
- Playwright for cross-browser testing
- Visual regression testing
- User journey testing
- CI/CD integration

## Deployment & DevOps

### Build Process
- TypeScript compilation
- ESLint code quality checks
- Asset optimization
- Environment-specific builds

### Hosting
- Static site deployment
- CDN for global distribution
- Environment variable management
- Monitoring and error tracking

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx` - React app initialization
- `src/App.tsx` - Main app component with routing
- `src/pages/Index.tsx` - Home page component

### Core Components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx` - Logo component with variants
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx` - Main app layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx` - Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx` - Top navigation bar

### Business Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx` - Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts` - Permission checking utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts` - Common utility functions

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts` - Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts` - Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml` - Backend configuration

## Common Patterns & Conventions

### Naming Conventions
- PascalCase for components and types
- camelCase for variables and functions
- kebab-case for CSS classes and files
- UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants

### Code Organization
- One component per file
- Related components in feature folders
- Shared utilities in lib folder
- Type definitions in types folder

### Git Workflow
- Feature branches from main
- Pull requests for code review
- Semantic commit messages
- Automated testing on PR

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the Beudox HR codebase and making informed decisions about modifications and extensions.
